title: Dictionary Comprehensions Practice
instructions: Practice creating and transforming dictionaries using comprehensions with filters and conditions.
hints: Use comprehension: {x: x**2 for x in range(1, n+1)}
Filter with condition: {k: v for k, v in d.items() if v >= min_value}
Use k.upper() if isinstance(k, str) else k for keys
This requires loop with setdefault or defaultdict, not pure comprehension

testCases.description: square_dict should map numbers 1 through n to their squares
square_dict of 1 should return a single mapping
filter_by_value should keep only items with value >= min_value
uppercase_keys should convert string keys to uppercase
testCases.validation: assert uppercase_keys({'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30}) == {'NAME': 'Alice', 'AGE': 30}, 'uppercase_keys should uppercase all string keys'

testCases.description: group_by_length should group words into lists by their length
testCases.validation: assert group_by_length(['hi', 'hey', 'hello', 'go']) == {2: ['hi', 'go'], 3: ['hey'], 5: ['hello']}, 'group_by_length should group words by length'

testCases.description: group_by_length should return empty dict for empty list

description: Practice dictionary comprehensions to build and transform dictionaries concisely. Create mappings of numbers to their squares, filter dictionaries by value thresholds, transform keys with conditional expressions, and group items by computed properties. These techniques enable expressive and efficient dictionary construction in Python.
